title: Vela Trajectory v0.1.0
description: First-class kernel object for the search path that produced (or did not produce) a finding. The eighth essay primitive: 'so the next agent does not re-derive what the last one already ruled out.' Steps are append-only via `trajectory.step_appended` events; the materialized `steps` collection is reproduced by replay from genesis.

id.description: Content-addressed identifier. First 16 hex chars of SHA-256(sorted_target_findings.join(',') | deposited_by | created), prefixed with 'vtr_'. Steps are NOT folded into the id.

target_findings.description: vf_* findings this trajectory describes the search for. May be empty when the trajectory leads nowhere yet.

deposited_by.description: Stable actor id of the depositor (lab / curator / agent that opens the trajectory).

created.description: RFC 3339 creation timestamp (also folded into the id).

steps.description: Append-only ordered list of steps. Reproduced by replay from `trajectory.step_appended` events.

id.description: Content-addressed step id: SHA-256(parent_trajectory_id | kind.canonical() | normalize(description) | at | actor) prefixed with 'vts_'.

kind.description: What kind of step this is. `ruled_out` is the most information-dense for downstream agents.

description.description: Free-text description. For `ruled_out`, prose should name the reason for exclusion.

actor.description: Stable actor id of who took the step. May differ from the trajectory's deposited_by.

references.description: Optional referenced kernel objects (vf_*, vnr_*, vrep_*, vpred_*, vd_*, vc_*).

notes.description: Free-text reviewer note on the trajectory as a whole.
